cap_space_post_fa: TBD — restructures/cuts required to absorb Phillips/Lloyd deals; team entered FA with ~$7.3M-$9M per Spotrac/OTC

key_departures.impact: Starting center who took over the role and played well enough to push Austin Corbett to guard. His departure immediately created a hole at center.
Back-to-back 1,000-yard rusher who was the team's lead back in 2025 with 1,076 yards. Chuba Hubbard (under contract) remains but this thins the backfield and removes the RB1.
Rotational edge rusher who was part of a thin pass-rush group. His departure compounds an already pre-existing EDGE need that the team addressed via signing Jaelan Phillips.
Rotational edge rusher depth. Depth loss, but Phillips signing more than compensates.
Was the starting ILB but struggled in pass coverage (allowed 56 catches, 560 yards, 3 TDs per PFF). His departure is offset by the Devin Lloyd signing — actually an upgrade.
Starting safety lost to free agency. No replacement signed yet. Creates/escalates a safety need.
Interior lineman who played guard and was a depth/swing option. Part of a broader IOL attrition — compounds the center problem.
Backup OT who served as depth behind Ikem Ekwonu. With Ekwonu out for the season or most of it (torn patellar tendon), Nijman's departure leaves a big gap at LT.

signings.analysis: Elite-market signing at $30M AAV (between Josh Allen/Hines-Allen and Nick Bosa on the pay scale). Phillips ranked 9th in the NFL in QB pressures (73) in 2025 and is only 26 years old. 4 years/$120M with $80M guaranteed is a full commitment — this is their guy. EDGE drops from a critical tier-1 need to a depth need (developmental day 3 pick at most). The team was dead last in pressure rate (26.2%) in 2025; this is the centerpiece fix.

Second-team All-Pro in 2025 (81 tackles, 5 INTs including a 99-yard TD return). Pro Bowl selection. This is top-4 AAV among off-ball LBs. $25M guaranteed on a 3-year deal is a real commitment. GM Dan Morgan (a former MLB himself) explicitly targeted this position — Lloyd will be the defensive signal-caller and green dot. Replaces and massively upgrades over Rozeboom, who struggled badly in coverage. LB drops from a tier-1 critical need to fully addressed.

One-year deal to replace Andy Dalton as Bryce Young's backup. Former 2022 first-round pick (20th overall) with a 16-11 career record as a starter. Provides a younger, more athletic backup option. This is a backup signing — it does NOT change QB depth tier meaningfully for the draft but does address the immediate backup need. Bryce Young remains the unquestioned QB1; Young's extension question is still unresolved long-term.

1-year deal worth up to $4.75M. Will be penciled in as starting center after Cade Mays left for Detroit. Good pass blocker (allowed only 3 sacks in 2025). Per ESPN, the team does not view him as a long-term solution — expect them to draft a center to eventually replace him. Minimally addresses center need; does not solve it long-term. IOL remains a priority in the draft.

1-year deal. 27 career starts in 5 seasons. Will serve as the swing tackle while Ikem Ekwonu (torn patellar tendon) recovers. PFF ranked him 72nd of 84 qualifying tackles last year. This is insurance depth, not a starter-quality answer. Starting LT remains a major need through the draft — Ekwonu may miss all or most of 2026. OT remains a top draft priority.

Restricted free agent re-signed. Key rotational DE — had 5.5 sacks in 2024 filling in for Derrick Brown. Now part of an EDGE room that has Phillips at the top. Depth retention; no net change to needs.
1-year deal. Re-signed depth CB. Was signed off Cowboys practice squad mid-2025, appeared in 4 regular-season games and wild card game. Special teams contributor. No impact on starting CB need.
1-year deal to return. Depth CB retention. Jaycee Horn is the clear CB1; Evans and Rochell provide low-cost depth behind him. CB depth is fine; CB2 opposite Horn is the only remaining moderate need.

Re-signed versatile LB/S hybrid. Provides flexibility in the secondary and can play multiple positions. Does not replace Nick Scott at free safety — S remains a need. His versatility is valuable depth but this is not a starting safety acquisition.

Re-signed as a veteran depth piece and mentor. Missed most of 2025 with an elbow injury. The WR room is anchored by Tetairoa McMillan (rookie of year-caliber 2025), Jalen Coker, and Xavier Legette. Moore is low-cost depth with value in the locker room under HC Dave Canales (their third team together). No impact on WR need tier.
Restricted free agent re-signed. Young developmental EDGE. With Phillips added, Incoom is now EDGE depth/developmental. No change to needs.
Re-signed for a record 18th season as long snapper. Legendary franchise continuity signing. No impact on positional needs.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Jaelan Phillips (4yr/$120M, $80M gtd) is an elite commitment at $30M AAV. He ranked 9th in pressures in 2025 and is only 26. With LaBryan Ray and Thomas Incoom re-signed as depth, this group is set. EDGE is done as a premium need — developmental day 3 pick acceptable, no longer a top-2 round target.

Devin Lloyd (3yr/$45M, $25M gtd) was the top LB on the market, made second-team All-Pro in 2025 with 5 INTs. GM Morgan explicitly prioritized this — and landed his guy. The one upgrade that could have been made was Tremaine Edmunds as a partner, but Lloyd alone eliminates this as a draft need. LB is not a top-3 round target now.

Ikem Ekwonu (torn patellar tendon) is likely to miss most or all of 2026. Yosh Nijman, the backup LT, departed in free agency. Stone Forsythe (1-year depth deal, PFF-ranked 72nd of 84 tackles) is not a real answer at LT. This is now a critical need — starting LT is unavailable. OT should be the top offensive priority in the draft at pick 19.

Cade Mays (starting C) departed for Detroit. Luke Fortner (1yr/$4.75M) is a short-term bridge, not a long-term solution. Austin Corbett also left. Fortner slightly reduces urgency (they have a body at C), but team explicitly expects to draft the long-term center. IOL stays as a round 2-3 priority. Need is partially addressed but not resolved.

Nick Scott departed (starting free safety, unsigned). Isaiah Simmons re-signed but is a hybrid LB/S who does not solve the traditional FS role. No true starting safety has been added. Safety remains a round 2-4 need. The departure of Scott and lack of a real replacement keeps this elevated.

Rico Dowdle (back-to-back 1,000-yard rusher) departed to Pittsburgh. Chuba Hubbard (4yr/$33.2M extension) remains as the RB1 but was surpassed by Dowdle on the depth chart in 2025 after a calf injury. Jonathan Brooks and Trevor Etienne are developmental backups. The backfield needs a proven RB2/complementary back. RB escalates from a moderate need to a genuine round 2-4 target.

Kenny Pickett (1yr/$7.5M) replaces Andy Dalton as backup. This is a serviceable improvement at the backup spot but does not change the long-term QB picture. Bryce Young is the franchise QB entering year 4. No draft capital needed at QB unless a value pick emerges.

No moves at TE. James Mitchell (restricted FA) has not been reported as re-signed. TE remains a legitimate round 2-4 target — the team lacks a proven receiving threat at the position. Unchanged from pre-FA assessment.
Jaycee Horn is the locked-in CB1. Akayleb Evans and Robert Rochell re-signed as 1-year depth pieces. Damarri Mathis departed. A CB2 opposite Horn is still a round 2-4 need. Unchanged.

key_additions.headline: Elite pass rusher (9th in NFL pressures, 2025) addresses Carolina's biggest defensive need on a max-tier deal
Second-team All-Pro linebacker (5 INTs in 2025) immediately becomes the defensive signal-caller and green dot
Former first-round pick replaces Andy Dalton as Bryce Young's backup with starting experience
Bridge center fills immediate void left by Cade Mays' departure, with draft likely to provide long-term answer
Swing tackle depth acquired as insurance while Ikem Ekwonu recovers from torn patellar tendon

key_losses.headline: Starting center departs for Detroit, creating immediate void addressed only by a short-term bridge signing
Back-to-back 1,000-yard rusher leaves for Pittsburgh, thinning an already thin backfield behind Chuba Hubbard
Starting free safety departs unsigned, escalating a safety need with no proven replacement added
Backup LT departs as Ekwonu recovery leaves Panthers without a reliable starting tackle — critical OT need now exists
Starting ILB departs but is directly replaced and massively upgraded by Devin Lloyd signing

notes: Stone Forsythe contract terms not yet publicly disclosed beyond 1-year confirmation. LaBryan Ray, Isaiah Simmons, David Moore re-sign terms also not fully reported — assessed as low-cost depth deals. All major signings (Phillips, Lloyd, Pickett, Fortner) are confirmed with full contract structure from multiple Tier 1 sources (NFL.com, ESPN, Spotrac). Cap space figures reflect pre-restructure reality — team will need moves to absorb Phillips/Lloyd deals.
